Tool claude Runs locally using your own API token. No data sent to vibe-replay servers.
I think we can make this clearer here, to build user trust

Let me think about what builds trust here. Users need to understand: How it works
mechanically — it spawns their local CLI tool in headless mode What "your own token" means —
uses whatever API key they've already configured Privacy guarantee — session data stays on
their machine, never touches our servers Cost implication — it consumes tokens from their
account Now update the null state too — same trust-building language: Clean. Let me commit:
Now the tool status area displays three clear trust signals: Runs claude in headless mode on
your machine — explicitly says it runs locally, session data doesn't leave localhost Uses the
API key already configured in claude — clarifies it uses the user's own key, costs are charge…
